root_text.an5.97:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu ānāpānassatiṁ bhāvento nacirasseva akuppaṁ paṭivijjhati.
root_text.an5.97:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu appaṭṭho hoti appakicco subharo susantoso jīvitaparikkhāresu;
root_text.an5.97:1.7: appicchakathā …pe… vimuttiñāṇadassanakathā, evarūpiyā kathāya nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī;
root_text.an5.97:1.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu ānāpānassatiṁ bhāvento nacirasseva akuppaṁ paṭivijjhatī”ti.

translation_text.an5.97:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant developing mindfulness of breathing who has five things will soon penetrate the unshakable.
translation_text.an5.97:1.3: It’s when a mendicant has few concerns and duties, and is unburdensome and contented with life’s necessities.
translation_text.an5.97:1.4: They eat little, not devoted to filling their stomach.
translation_text.an5.97:1.5: They are rarely drowsy, and are dedicated to wakefulness.
translation_text.an5.97:1.6: They get to take part in talk about self-effacement that helps open the heart, when they want, without trouble or difficulty. That is,
translation_text.an5.97:1.7: talk about fewness of wishes, contentment, seclusion, keeping your distance, arousing energy, ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, and the knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an5.97:1.8: They review the extent of their mind’s freedom.
translation_text.an5.97:1.9: A mendicant developing mindfulness of breathing who has these five things will soon penetrate the unshakable.”

reference_text.an5.97:1.1: csp1ed16.106, csp2ed16.106, ms16A5_585, msdiv97, ndp17.378, pts-vp-pli3.121, vri37.113
